risk_title: Government Capacity Constraints and Institutional Fragility in Project Execution
risk_summary: The Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security (MAFS) has demonstrated persistent weak institutional capacity affecting project implementation, with internal fiduciary controls showing no improvement since the last supervision mission and audit findings of ineligible expenditures.
evidence_quote: At the project design stage, the ministry's capacity was noted as being very weak. The assessment report identified the ministry's capacity gaps and made recommendations for MAFS capacity building, which the project will support for the next 12 months.

risk_title: Delayed Government Endorsement of Critical Food Security Policy Framework
risk_summary: The Food Security Crisis Preparedness Plan (FSCPP), a key covenant requirement, remains pending presidential decree approval despite Council of Ministers approval, creating uncertainty in the government's institutional readiness to implement food security mechanisms.
evidence_quote: One key dated covenant that has not yet been fulfilled is the establishment of the Food Security Crisis Preparedness Plan (FSCPP) acceptable to the World Bank... The Bank is waiting for the final endorsement of the Plan by the President's office.

risk_title: Loss of Project Funds Due to Government Administrative Failure
risk_summary: The government failed to request an extension of the FS2030 Trust Fund before its closing date, resulting in loss of US$0.5 million in project resources and requiring refund of US$250,000 in unutilized advances.
evidence_quote: Under the AF, the project lost US$0.5 million due to the government inability to request an extension of the TF before its closing date. As a result, the project is required to refund US$250,000 that has been advanced to the designated account but remained unutilized.

risk_title: Weak Project Management and Oversight Systems
risk_summary: The Single Project Coordination Unit (SPCU) demonstrates systemic weaknesses in field mission management, financial oversight, and third-party monitoring quality, with ad hoc rather than structured planning affecting implementation effectiveness.
evidence_quote: The SPCU has shown weaknesses in managing Field missions and overseas training. Field trips are often conducted without clear terms of reference and the resulting back-to-office reports lack sufficient detail... the quality of this first report was not acceptable and SPCU was advised to carefully review the accuracy and completeness of the information before submitting it to the World Bank.

risk_title: Unresolved Audit Findings and Financial Management Deficiencies
risk_summary: The 2023/2024 annual audit identified ineligible expenditures of US$13,700 requiring reimbursement, and internal fiduciary control systems continue to show several weaknesses with no improvement since the last supervision mission.
evidence_quote: Internal fiduciary control systems still show several weaknesses, indicating that it has not improved since the last supervision mission. The 2023/2024 annual audit uncovered ineligible expenditures amounting to US$13,700. As recommended by the audit report, this amount needs to be reimbursed to the World Bank no later than July 15, 2025.
